title: thlaspi sylvestre subsp. vogesiacum
intro: Thlaspi sylvestre subsp. vogesiacum, also known as Alpine Pennycress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, native to the Vosges Mountains in northeastern France. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its high content of glucosinolates, compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Alpine Pennycress include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible role in preventing certain types of cancer. Alpine Pennycress can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, tincture, or capsule, and is often used as a dietary supplement due to its potential health benefits.
